title: SUB GEOGRAPHY TOPIC 1 MONSOON 2 FOREST GRASSLANDS

questions.question: The term 'Monsoon' is derived from which language? / 'मानसून' शब्द की उत्पत्ति किस भाषा से हुई है?
questions.explanation: Monsoon is derived from the Arabic word 'Mausim', meaning seasonal reversal of winds.

questions.question: Which line of latitude divides India into tropical and subtropical zones? / कौन सी अक्षांश रेखा भारत को उष्णकटिबंधीय और उपोष्णकटिबंधीय क्षेत्रों में विभाजित करती है?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through the middle of India, affecting its climate significantly.

questions.question: What is the average period considered for determining climate? / जलवायु निर्धारण के लिए मानी जाने वाली औसत अवधि क्या है?
options: 30 years or more / 30 वर्ष या अधिक
questions.explanation: Climate is the average weather of a place over a long period, usually 30 years or more.

questions.question: Which factor causes the temperature to decrease as height increases? / ऊंचाई बढ़ने पर तापमान कम होने का कारण कौन सा कारक है?
options: Distance from sea / समुद्र से दूरी
questions.explanation: As altitude increases, the atmosphere becomes thinner, leading to lower temperatures.

questions.question: What is the local name for pre-monsoon showers in West Bengal? / पश्चिम बंगाल में मानसून-पूर्व वर्षा का स्थानीय नाम क्या है?
questions.explanation: Kal Baisakhi are violent thunderstorms in West Bengal helpful for jute and tea.

questions.question: Which place in India receives the world's highest average rainfall? / भारत के किस स्थान पर विश्व की सर्वाधिक औसत वर्षा होती है?
questions.explanation: Mawsynram, located in the Khasi Hills of Meghalaya, is the wettest place on Earth.

questions.question: Pre-monsoon showers in Kerala that help ripen mangoes are called? / केरल में मानसून-पूर्व वर्षा, जो आमों को पकने में मदद करती है, कहलाती है?
questions.explanation: Mango showers are pre-monsoon rains that assist in the early ripening of mangoes.

questions.question: What does ITCZ stand for? / ITCZ का पूर्ण रूप क्या है?
questions.explanation: ITCZ is a low-pressure zone near the equator where trade winds converge.

questions.question: In which month does the ITCZ shift over the Ganga plain? / किस महीने में ITCZ गंगा के मैदान के ऊपर खिसक जाता है?
questions.explanation: In July, the ITCZ shifts to about 20°N-25°N over the Gangetic plain.

questions.question: Which plateau gets intensely heated during summer to aid monsoon? / गर्मियों के दौरान कौन सा पठार अत्यधिक गर्म होकर मानसून में सहायता करता है?
options: Chota Nagpur Plateau / छोटा नागपुर पठार
questions.explanation: Intense heating of the Tibetan Plateau creates strong vertical air currents and low pressure.

questions.question: The Southwest Monsoon typically arrives in Kerala by? / दक्षिण-पश्चिम मानसून आमतौर पर केरल में कब तक आता है?
questions.explanation: The monsoon onset usually occurs in the first week of June on the Kerala coast.

questions.question: Which branch of the monsoon brings rainfall to the Mumbai coast? / मानसून की कौन सी शाखा मुंबई तट पर वर्षा लाती है?
options: Bay of Bengal branch / बंगाल की खाड़ी शाखा
questions.explanation: The Arabian Sea branch of the Southwest monsoon hits the Western Ghats and Mumbai.

questions.question: What is the phenomenon of a rainless interval during monsoon called? / मानसून के दौरान वर्षा रहित अंतराल की घटना को क्या कहा जाता है?
options: Break in monsoon / मानसून में विच्छेद
questions.explanation: 'Break' in monsoon refers to the dry periods between rainy spells.

questions.question: 'October Heat' is caused by? / 'अक्टूबर हीट' किसके कारण होती है?
options: High temperature and high humidity / उच्च तापमान और उच्च आर्द्रता
questions.explanation: High temperature and humidity make the weather oppressive during the retreat of monsoon.

questions.question: Where does the Retreating Monsoon bring significant rainfall? / लौटता हुआ मानसून कहाँ महत्वपूर्ण वर्षा लाता है?
questions.explanation: The Coromandel coast (Tamil Nadu) receives most of its rain from the retreating monsoon.

questions.question: What is the Spanish meaning of 'El Niño'? / 'एल नीनो' का स्पेनिश अर्थ क्या है?
questions.explanation: El Niño means 'The Child', referring to a warm ocean current appearing off Peru.

questions.question: How does El Niño affect the Indian Monsoon? / एल नीनो भारतीय मानसून को कैसे प्रभावित करता है?
options: Causes heavy snow / भारी हिमपात का कारण बनता है
Causes drought or weak monsoon / सूखे या कमजोर मानसून का कारण बनता है
questions.explanation: El Niño generally has a negative impact, leading to weaker monsoon rains in India.

questions.question: What is the 'Cold Phase' of the ENSO cycle called? / ENSO चक्र के 'ठंडे चरण' को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: La Niña represents the cooling of sea surface temperatures in the tropical Pacific.

questions.question: Positive IOD (Indian Ocean Dipole) results in? / सकारात्मक IOD (हिंद महासागर द्विध्रुव) का परिणाम क्या होता है?
options: Cyclones in Pacific / प्रशांत क्षेत्र में चक्रवात
Drought in India / भारत में सूखा
Better monsoon rainfall in India / भारत में बेहतर मानसून वर्षा
questions.explanation: Positive IOD helps in bringing more rainfall to the Indian subcontinent.

questions.question: Which winds are responsible for winter rain in Northwest India? / उत्तर-पश्चिम भारत में सर्दियों की वर्षा के लिए कौन सी हवाएँ जिम्मेदार हैं?
questions.explanation: Western disturbances originating from the Mediterranean bring rain to Punjab and Haryana.

questions.question: Jet streams are found in which layer of the atmosphere? / जेट धाराएँ वायुमंडल की किस परत में पाई जाती हैं?
questions.explanation: Jet streams are fast-flowing, narrow air currents found in the upper troposphere.

questions.question: The sudden increase in rainfall with thunder and lightning is called? / गरज और बिजली के साथ वर्षा में अचानक वृद्धि को क्या कहा जाता है?
options: Burst of monsoon / मानसून का फटना
questions.explanation: The sudden onset of heavy rainfall marks the 'burst' of the monsoon.

questions.question: Which state is the first to experience the retreating monsoon? / लौटते हुए मानसून का अनुभव करने वाला पहला राज्य कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: The withdrawal of the monsoon begins from the northwestern states of India in September.

questions.question: MJO (Madden-Julian Oscillation) travels in which direction? / MJO (मैडेन-जूलियन ऑसिलेशन) किस दिशा में यात्रा करता है?
options: North to South / उत्तर से दक्षिण
South to North / दक्षिण से उत्तर
West to East / पश्चिम से पूर्व
East to West / पूर्व से पश्चिम
questions.explanation: MJO is an eastward-moving disturbance of clouds and rain near the equator.

questions.question: Which region is in the 'Rain Shadow' of the Western Ghats? / कौन सा क्षेत्र पश्चिमी घाट के 'वृष्टि छाया' क्षेत्र में आता है?
questions.explanation: The eastern side of Western Ghats receives less rain as it lies in the rain shadow area.

questions.question: What causes the seasonal reversal of wind direction in India? / भारत में हवा की दिशा के मौसमी उलटफेर का कारण क्या है?
questions.explanation: Differential heating of land and water creates pressure gradients causing wind reversal.

questions.question: 'Bordoisila' is the local name for pre-monsoon storms in? / 'बोरदोईसिला' कहाँ के मानसून-पूर्व तूफानों का स्थानीय नाम है?
questions.explanation: Bordoisila are thunderous rainfall events occurring in Assam before the monsoon.

questions.question: The Southern Oscillation refers to pressure changes in which ocean? / दक्षिणी दोलन किस महासागर में दबाव परिवर्तन को संदर्भित करता है?
questions.explanation: It involves the see-saw pattern of atmospheric pressure between the Pacific and Indian Oceans.

questions.question: Winter rainfall in Tamil Nadu is mainly due to? / तमिलनाडु में सर्दियों की वर्षा मुख्य रूप से किसके कारण होती है?
questions.explanation: The North-East monsoon (Retreating monsoon) picks up moisture from the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Which ministry issues the official monsoon forecast in India? / भारत में कौन सा मंत्रालय आधिकारिक मानसून पूर्वानुमान जारी करता है?
options: Ministry of Home Affairs / गृह मंत्रालय
Ministry of Earth Sciences / पृथ्वी विज्ञान मंत्रालय
questions.explanation: The IMD, under the Ministry of Earth Sciences, provides all weather and monsoon data.

questions.question: How many broad groups is Indian vegetation divided into? / भारतीय वनस्पति को मोटे तौर पर कितने समूहों में विभाजित किया गया है?
questions.explanation: Indian vegetation is categorized into 5 major groups including Evergreen and Deciduous forests.

questions.question: Which forest type is also known as "Rainforests"? / किस प्रकार के वनों को "वर्षा वन" भी कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Evergreen forests are called rainforests as they remain green year-round in high rainfall areas.

questions.question: What is the minimum annual rainfall required for Evergreen forests? / सदाबहार वनों के लिए न्यूनतम वार्षिक वर्षा कितनी आवश्यक है?
questions.explanation: These forests thrive in areas with more than 200 cm of rainfall and temperatures above 22°C.

questions.question: Why are Evergreen forests called "Deserts covered with trees"? / सदाबहार वनों को "वृक्षों से ढके रेगिस्तान" क्यों कहा जाता है?
options: High salt content / उच्च नमक सामग्री
No sunlight / सूर्य का प्रकाश न होना
Lack of water / पानी की कमी
Low humus due to rapid decomposition / तीव्र अपघटन के कारण कम ह्यूमस
questions.explanation: Rapid decomposition of organic matter leads to low humus levels in the soil of these forests.

questions.question: Which tree bark is used for treating Malaria? / मलेरिया के उपचार के लिए किस पेड़ की छाल का उपयोग किया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Cinchona bark contains quinine, which is primarily used for the prevention and cure of Malaria.

questions.question: Which are the most widespread forests in India? / भारत में सबसे व्यापक रूप से फैले हुए वन कौन से हैं?
questions.explanation: Deciduous forests, also known as Monsoon forests, are the most common forest type in India.

questions.question: What is the rainfall range for Deciduous forests? / पर्णपाती वनों के लिए वर्षा का दायरा क्या है?
questions.explanation: These forests are spread across regions receiving between 70 cm and 200 cm of annual rainfall.

questions.question: For how many weeks do Deciduous trees shed leaves in summer? / पर्णपाती वृक्ष गर्मियों में कितने सप्ताह के लिए अपनी पत्तियाँ गिराते हैं?
questions.explanation: Deciduous trees shed their leaves for about 6 to 8 weeks during the dry summer season.

questions.question: Which leaves are used to make 'Bidi'? / 'बीड़ी' बनाने के लिए किन पत्तियों का उपयोग किया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Tendu leaves are commercially harvested and used as wrappers for making bidis.

questions.question: Dry Deciduous forests are also known as? / शुष्क पर्णपाती वनों को और किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Dry deciduous forests are often referred to as Parkland landscapes.

questions.question: Thorn forests are found in areas with rainfall less than? / काँटेदार वन उन क्षेत्रों में पाए जाते हैं जहाँ वर्षा कितने से कम होती है?
questions.explanation: Thorn forests are found in semi-arid regions receiving less than 50 cm of annual rainfall.

questions.question: Why do plants in thorn forests have thorns? / काँटेदार वनों के पौधों में काँटे क्यों होते हैं?
options: To prevent water loss / जल हानि से बचने के लिए
For better growth / बेहतर विकास के लिए
To attract insects / कीड़ों को आकर्षित करने के लिए
To absorb sunlight / सूर्य के प्रकाश को सोखने के लिए
questions.explanation: Thorns are modified leaves designed to reduce transpiration and conserve water.

questions.question: What is "Tussocky grass"? / "गुच्छ/त्सॉकी घास" क्या है?
options: Grass of the Tundra / टुंड्रा की घास
A type of moss / एक प्रकार की काई
Grass found in thorn forests (up to 2m) / काँटेदार वनों में पाई जाने वाली घास
A type of aquatic plant / एक प्रकार का जलीय पौधा
questions.explanation: Tussocky grass is a special variety found in thorn forests reaching heights of about 2 meters.

questions.question: Which forest is also known as Boreal Forest? / किस वन को बोरियल वन के नाम से भी जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Taiga forests are cold, sub-arctic coniferous forests also known as Boreal forests.

questions.question: Where are Coniferous forests found in India? / भारत में शंकुधारी वन कहाँ पाए जाते हैं?
questions.explanation: Coniferous forests with cone-shaped trees are found in high altitude regions like the Himalayas.

questions.question: Which tribes use Montane forests for grazing? / कौन सी जनजातियाँ चराई के लिए पर्वतीय वनों का उपयोग करती हैं?
options: Bhil and Gond / भील और गोंड
Guijar and Bakarwal / गुज्जर और बकरवाल
questions.explanation: Nomadic tribes like Gujjars and Bakarwals use high-altitude grasslands for cattle grazing.

questions.question: What are the temperate grasslands of the Nilgiris called? / नीलगिरि के समशीतोष्ण घास के मैदानों को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Temperate grasslands/forests in the Nilgiris and Anaimalai hills are locally known as Sholas.

questions.question: Mangrove forests are also known as? / मैंग्रोव वनों को और किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Mangroves are coastal forests influenced by tides, often called swamp or tidal forests.

questions.question: In which delta is the "Sundari" tree found? / "सुंदरी" वृक्ष किस डेल्टा में पाया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Sundari trees are the dominant species in the Sundarban delta of West Bengal.

questions.question: When was the first India State of Forest Report (ISFR) published? / पहली भारतीय वन स्थिति रिपोर्ट कब प्रकाशित हुई थी?
questions.explanation: The FSI started publishing the biennial India State of Forest Report in 1987.

questions.question: According to ISFR 2023, what is India's total Forest + Tree cover? / ISFR 2023 के अनुसार भारत का कुल वन + वृक्ष आच्छादन कितना है?
questions.explanation: As per the 2023 report, the combined forest and tree cover stands at 25.17%.

questions.question: Which state has the largest forest cover by area? / क्षेत्रफल के अनुसार किस राज्य में सर्वाधिक वन आच्छादन है?
questions.explanation: Madhya Pradesh has the largest forest area in India, followed by Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.question: Which UT/State has the highest forest cover by percentage? / प्रतिशत के अनुसार किस केंद्र शासित प्रदेश/राज्य में सर्वाधिक वन आच्छादन है?
questions.explanation: Lakshadweep has the highest percentage of forest cover, followed by Mizoram.

questions.question: What target for forest cover was set by the National Forest Policy (1988)? / राष्ट्रीय वन नीति (1988) द्वारा वन आच्छादन का क्या लक्ष्य रखा गया था?
questions.explanation: The policy mandates that 33% of the total geographical area should be under forest or tree cover.

questions.question: What is the "Miyawaki" method used for? / "मियावाकी" विधि का उपयोग किसके लिए किया जाता है?
options: Creating dense urban forests / सघन शहरी वन बनाना
Controlling forest fires / जंगल की आग को नियंत्रित करना
questions.explanation: Developed by Akira Miyawaki, this method creates dense, multi-layered self-sustaining forests.

questions.question: When did the Chipko Movement start? / चिपको आंदोलन कब शुरू हुआ था?
questions.explanation: The Chipko movement began in 1973 in the Chamoli district of Uttarakhand.

questions.question: Who led the Chipko Movement? / चिपको आंदोलन का नेतृत्व किसने किया?
questions.explanation: Sunderlal Bahuguna led the movement to stop commercial logging through non-violent protest.

questions.question: When is World Forest Day celebrated? / विश्व वन दिवस कब मनाया जाता है?
questions.explanation: International Day of Forests is observed annually on 21 March to raise awareness about forests.

questions.question: Where is the Forest Research Institute of India located? / भारतीय वन अनुसंधान संस्थान कहाँ स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The Forest Research Institute (FRI) is a premier institution located in Dehradun, Uttarakhand.

questions.question: What are the temperate grasslands of North America called? / उत्तरी अमेरिका के समशीतोष्ण घास के मैदानों को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Prairies are the temperate grasslands of North America, known as the "Granary of the world."
